levels.label: Cost-Plus
levels.copy: The Pricing & Monetisation Maturity pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Emerging
levels.copy: Some Pricing & Monetisation Maturity practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Structured
levels.copy: Core Pricing & Monetisation Maturity practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Value-Based
levels.copy: Pricing & Monetisation Maturity practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Monetisation Engine
levels.copy: Pricing & Monetisation Maturity is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.value-understanding: Interview recent wins, losses, and churned customers about alternatives, outcomes, budget, and trade-offs.
recommendations.packaging-and-segmentation: Redesign packages around distinct use cases and value, with explicit upgrade paths and fences.
recommendations.price-architecture-and-governance: Evaluate candidate value metrics and establish discount, approval, and exception governance.
recommendations.experimentation-and-realisation: Create a quarterly pricing research and experimentation roadmap with cohort guardrails.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with two questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
